dora_requirement_summary: Management body responsibility for ICT risk framework — board approval and personal accountability
uk_orf_reference: PRA SS1/21 §3.4 — board responsible for operational resilience
gap_description: ISO 27001 requires top management commitment but not board-level personal accountability for ICT risk. DORA Art. 5 is explicit.
recommended_action: Formalise ICT risk as a named board agenda item. Assign named board member accountability. Document board ICT competency.

dora_requirement_summary: Management body responsibility for ICT risk framework — board approval and personal accountability
gap_description: No management body ICT training requirement in ISO 27001.
recommended_action: Document board ICT training or competency assessment.

dora_requirement_summary: Management body ICT competency and training
iso27001_control_name: Information security awareness education and training
gap_description: ISO 27001 covers staff awareness training but not board-level ICT competency.
recommended_action: Establish board ICT awareness programme and evidence completion.

dora_requirement_summary: ICT risk management framework — documented and reviewed at least annually
gap_description: ISO 27001 review frequency is not mandated. DORA requires annual minimum review and event-triggered review after major incidents.
recommended_action: Establish an annual DORA-specific review cycle independent of the ISO 27001 management review schedule.

dora_requirement_summary: Digital operational resilience strategy — separately named board-endorsed document
gap_description: ISO 27001 does not require a separately named digital operational resilience strategy.
recommended_action: Create a Digital Operational Resilience Strategy document endorsed by the management body.

dora_requirement_summary: ICT system capacity and performance management
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.6 covers capacity management.
recommended_action: No action required for firms with mature A.8.6 implementations.

dora_requirement_summary: Legacy system risk identification and documentation
gap_description: ISO 27001 covers vulnerability management but not end-of-life system lifecycle risk specifically.
recommended_action: Flag end-of-life systems in asset inventory. Document risk and compensating controls for each.

dora_requirement_summary: ICT system identification classification and mapping of data flows and dependencies
iso27001_control_name: Inventory of information and other associated assets
uk_orf_reference: FCA PS21/3 / PRA SS1/21 — important business service mapping
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.9 requires an asset inventory. UK ORF requires IBS mapping. DORA requires extension to data flow and dependency mapping at ICT layer.
recommended_action: Extend asset inventory to include data flows and ICT dependencies for critical functions.

dora_requirement_summary: ICT security policies — protection prevention access management encryption
gap_description: ISO 27001 policy framework provides broad coverage but DORA Art. 9 prescribes policy content more specifically.
recommended_action: Review policy suite against Art. 9 requirements and address any missing policy areas.

dora_requirement_summary: Access management and privileged access controls
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.15-A.5.18 and A.8.2 provide comprehensive access management coverage.
recommended_action: No action required for firms with mature access management controls.

dora_requirement_summary: Access management and privileged access controls
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.2 covers privileged access.
recommended_action: No action required.

dora_requirement_summary: Protection against malware
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.7 provides full coverage.
recommended_action: No action required.

dora_requirement_summary: ICT vulnerability and patch management
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.8 provides coverage. Verify patching timelines are documented for critical systems.
recommended_action: Document maximum acceptable patching windows for critical function systems.

dora_requirement_summary: Detection of anomalous activities — monitoring of all ICT infrastructure layers
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.15 and A.8.16 cover logging and monitoring. DORA requires comprehensive coverage across all critical function ICT layers.
recommended_action: Verify monitoring coverage extends to all systems supporting critical or important functions.

dora_requirement_summary: Detection of anomalous activities — monitoring of all ICT infrastructure layers
gap_description: See above.
recommended_action: See above.

dora_requirement_summary: Response and recovery plans — documented and tested with defined RTOs and RPOs
gap_description: ISO 27001 covers incident response. UK ORF adds scenario testing. Gap: DORA requires RTO/RPO at ICT function level.
recommended_action: Define and document RTO/RPO for each ICT system supporting a critical or important function.

dora_requirement_summary: Response and recovery plans — documented and tested with defined RTOs and RPOs
gap_description: UK ORF impact tolerances operate at business service level. DORA requires ICT-layer granularity.
recommended_action: Cascade UK ORF impact tolerances to ICT system RTOs.

dora_requirement_summary: Backup policies — isolation from production and annual restoration testing
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.13 covers backup policy. DORA requires documented isolation architecture and evidenced restoration testing.
recommended_action: Document backup isolation architecture. Establish annual restoration test schedule with evidence retention.

dora_requirement_summary: Redundancy of ICT infrastructure supporting critical functions
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.14 covers redundancy. DORA requires documentation per critical function.
recommended_action: Document redundancy architecture for each critical function system.

dora_requirement_summary: Post-incident learning and third-party threat intelligence integration
uk_orf_reference: PRA SS1/21 lessons from scenario testing
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.27 and A.5.7 together cover post-incident learning and threat intelligence. UK ORF adds scenario testing lessons.
recommended_action: No action required for firms with mature A.5.27 and A.5.7 implementations.

dora_requirement_summary: Post-incident learning and third-party threat intelligence integration
gap_description: See above.
recommended_action: See above.

dora_requirement_summary: Crisis communications plan for clients counterparties and public
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.26 covers incident response but not separate external crisis communication procedures.
recommended_action: Create a crisis communication plan covering external stakeholders. Name the ICT crisis communications role.

dora_requirement_summary: ICT-related incident management process with defined roles and escalation paths
iso27001_control_name: Information security incident management planning and preparation
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.24 provides good process coverage. DORA adds requirement to integrate with ICT risk management framework.
recommended_action: Confirm incident management process is formally integrated with the ICT risk management framework.

dora_requirement_summary: Internal information security event reporting mechanism
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.6.8 provides full coverage.
recommended_action: No action required.

dora_requirement_summary: Evidence collection and preservation for regulatory investigation
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.28 provides coverage.
recommended_action: No action required for firms with mature A.5.28 implementations.

dora_requirement_summary: Classification of major incidents using six prescribed criteria
iso27001_control_name: Assessment and decision on information security events
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.25 requires event assessment but does not prescribe the six DORA criteria. FCA uses unprescribed "material" threshold.
recommended_action: Build a classification procedure using the six Art. 18 criteria. Define firm-specific thresholds for each criterion.

dora_requirement_summary: Initial notification to competent authority within 4 hours of classification as major (24 hours from detection)
iso27001_control_name: Information security incident management planning and preparation
uk_orf_reference: FCA SUP 15.3 — notify immediately (no fixed deadline)
gap_description: ISO 27001 and FCA SUP 15.3 have no fixed-hour notification deadline. DORA creates a hard 4-hour window from classification.
recommended_action: Create Art. 19 notification workflow. Appoint named accountable owner. Develop pre-populated initial notification template. Establish out-of-hours escalation.

dora_requirement_summary: Intermediate report to competent authority within 72 hours of initial notification
iso27001_control_name: Information security incident management planning and preparation
gap_description: No equivalent 72-hour structured report exists in ISO 27001 or UK ORF.
recommended_action: Develop intermediate report template. Build 72-hour notification workflow.

dora_requirement_summary: Final report to competent authority within 1 month of resolution
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.27 covers post-incident learning. DORA's final report is a regulatory submission with prescribed content.
recommended_action: Develop final report template. Establish 1-month post-resolution submission workflow.

dora_requirement_summary: Voluntary notification of significant cyber threats
gap_description: Voluntary only. ISO 27001 A.5.7 covers threat intelligence.
recommended_action: Document policy for determining when voluntary notification is appropriate.

dora_requirement_summary: Digital operational resilience testing programme — formally documented board-endorsed risk-based
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.35 requires independent review. UK ORF requires scenario testing. Neither requires a standalone board-endorsed testing programme document.
recommended_action: Create a Digital Operational Resilience Testing Programme document. Define scope risk methodology frequency and governance.

dora_requirement_summary: Vulnerability assessments and scans for systems supporting critical functions
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.8.8 provides coverage.
recommended_action: Verify scanning scope covers all critical function systems.

dora_requirement_summary: Open source and software composition analysis
gap_description: ISO 27001 does not specifically require software composition analysis.
recommended_action: Implement SCA tooling for systems with open source components supporting critical functions. Include in testing programme.

dora_requirement_summary: Scenario-based testing of critical functions
gap_description: UK ORF scenario testing provides direct coverage. ISO 27001 A.5.29 adds BCM context.
recommended_action: Ensure scenario testing covers ICT layer not only business service layer.

dora_requirement_summary: Penetration testing of systems supporting critical functions
gap_description: ISO 27001 does not mandate penetration testing. CBEST applies to systemically important firms.
recommended_action: Document penetration testing frequency and scope for critical function systems. Include in testing programme.

dora_requirement_summary: Threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) at least every 3 years for qualifying entities
gap_description: No ISO 27001 equivalent to TLPT. CBEST is functional UK equivalent for qualifying firms.
recommended_action: Determine whether firm qualifies under Art. 26(8). If yes: establish TLPT programme or confirm CBEST equivalence with competent authority.

dora_requirement_summary: TLPT results and remediation plans shared with competent authority
gap_description: ISO 27001 does not require disclosure of test results to regulators.
recommended_action: Establish regulatory disclosure procedure for TLPT results. Ensure remediation plans are documented before submission.

dora_requirement_summary: Requirements for TLPT testers — independence competency professional indemnity
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.35 requires independence but not specific tester qualifications.
recommended_action: Verify Art. 27 compliance when commissioning external TLPT testers.

dora_requirement_summary: ICT third-party risk management strategy integrated into ICT risk management framework
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.19 requires supplier security policy. DORA requires a specific ICT TPRM strategy addressing concentration risk and exit planning.
recommended_action: Create ICT third-party risk management strategy as a named component of the ICT risk management framework.

dora_requirement_summary: Register of Information — all ICT third-party arrangements in ESA-prescribed format
iso27001_control_name: Inventory of information and other associated assets
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.9 asset inventory is not equivalent to the Register of Information in prescribed DORA format.
recommended_action: Build the Register of Information using ESA-prescribed format. Establish a maintenance process integrated with supplier onboarding.

dora_requirement_summary: Due diligence before entering ICT arrangements
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.19 and FCA SYSC 8 together provide strong due diligence coverage.
recommended_action: Verify due diligence process includes DORA-specific concentration risk assessment (Art. 29).

dora_requirement_summary: Concentration risk assessment at entity level for critical function dependencies
iso27001_control_name: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
gap_description: ISO 27001 does not explicitly require concentration risk assessment.
recommended_action: Define concentration risk assessment methodology. Document findings for critical function ICT dependencies.

dora_requirement_summary: Contractual service levels and performance targets
iso27001_control_name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.20 and FCA SYSC 8 together provide coverage.
recommended_action: No material gap for firms with comprehensive security schedules.

dora_requirement_summary: Contractual data location and processing jurisdiction provisions
iso27001_control_name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
uk_orf_reference: No direct UK ORF equivalent (UK GDPR Art. 28 covers data processor requirements)
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.20 and UK GDPR Art. 28 together cover data location provisions.
recommended_action: No material gap for firms with mature data governance.

dora_requirement_summary: Contractual competent authority access inspection and audit rights — and provider cooperation with competent authorities
iso27001_control_name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
gap_description: FCA SYSC 8 requires audit rights. DORA extends this to include explicit competent authority access rights in the contract. Many existing contracts do not include this.
recommended_action: Review critical function contracts for competent authority access provisions. Update contracts or security schedules.

dora_requirement_summary: Contractual termination rights and exit strategy including transition assistance
iso27001_control_name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
gap_description: FCA SYSC 8 requires exit strategies for material outsourcing. DORA extends to all critical function ICT arrangements with more specificity.
recommended_action: Review exit provisions in critical function contracts. Ensure transition assistance periods and data portability are covered.

dora_requirement_summary: Contractual sub-contracting conditions and change notification obligations
iso27001_control_name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.21 covers supply chain security. Sub-contractor change notification obligations may not be present in existing contracts.
recommended_action: Include sub-contractor change notification requirements in provider contracts.

dora_requirement_summary: Contractual incident notification obligations — provider must notify firm within window enabling Art. 19 compliance
iso27001_control_name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.20 covers notification obligations generally. DORA requires notification timing compatible with the 4-hour Art. 19 window.
recommended_action: Verify provider incident notification SLAs. Require notification within ≤2 hours of provider classification to enable firm's 4-hour submission.

dora_requirement_summary: Mandatory contract clauses cannot be waived — applies to hyperscale providers
iso27001_control_name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
gap_description: ISO 27001 does not address non-negotiability of contract clauses. Many hyperscale standard terms may not satisfy Art. 30.
recommended_action: Assess hyperscale cloud provider contracts against Art. 30 mandatory clauses. Negotiate supplementary agreements where needed.

dora_requirement_summary: Annual review of ICT third-party arrangements including criticality classification
iso27001_control_name: Monitoring review and change management of supplier services
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.22 and FCA SYSC 8 together provide coverage.
recommended_action: Verify annual review includes DORA classification assessment (critical vs. non-critical function).

dora_requirement_summary: Cloud service specific ICT security requirements
iso27001_control_name: Information security for use of cloud services
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.23 covers cloud security. DORA's Art. 30 mandatory clauses apply to cloud providers with no waiver.
recommended_action: Verify cloud provider contracts include all Art. 30 mandatory provisions. Supplement with data portability and exit clauses as needed.

dora_requirement_summary: Voluntary cyber threat intelligence sharing among financial entities
uk_orf_reference: NCSC CiSP; Cyber Defence Alliance; FS-ISAC
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.7 covers threat intelligence. Established UK sharing forums satisfy the intent of Art. 45.
recommended_action: No mandatory action. Document participation in sharing forums and policy for what is shared.

dora_requirement_summary: Voluntary cyber threat intelligence sharing — safeguards for personal data
iso27001_control_name: Privacy and protection of personal data
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.34 and UK GDPR Art. 6 together cover personal data safeguards in shared intelligence.
recommended_action: Ensure sharing policy explicitly addresses personal data handling.

dora_requirement_summary: Voluntary cyber threat intelligence sharing — safeguards for business confidentiality
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.14 and A.6.6 cover information transfer and confidentiality.
recommended_action: No material gap for firms participating in established forums with governance frameworks.

dora_requirement_summary: Notification of competent authorities of information sharing arrangements
gap_description: ISO 27001 A.5.5 covers authority contact. DORA requires notification to competent authority and ENISA when arrangements are established.
recommended_action: For firms with EU entities: notify relevant EU competent authority and ENISA when information sharing arrangements are established.
